key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 FCF ex-acquisitions 173.1 MSEK ÷ 17,165,756 shares (TEQ.ST/official-reports/year-end-reports/2026-02-14 - Year-End Report 2025 - Teqnion AB.pdf; cross-check operating CF 184.6 MSEK less maintenance capex per TEQ.ST/official-reports/annual-reports/2026-03-21 - Årsredovisning 2025.pdf Note 27)
Margin hold + bolt-on M&A; exit ~16x P/FCF vs ~15.7x spot on FY2025 FCF/sh

open_questions: Confirm live TEQ.ST price before acting on IRR.
Re-extract Q1 2026 interim (inventory tier, 0 chars) to filing-ground Q1 metrics.
Verify FCF ex-acquisitions bridge vs operating CF 184.6 MSEK in annual.
thesis: Teqnion AB (TEQ.ST, Nasdaq First North) is a Swedish industrial serial acquirer. **[Fact]** The parent owned **38** operating subsidiaries at year-end 2025 (30 prior year) across niche industrial niches in Nordics and the UK. **[Fact]** FY2025 net sales were **1,800.0 MSEK** (+15%); organic net sales **-5%** after deliberate exits of unprofitable units. **[Fact]** FY2025 EBITA was **203.1 MSEK** (+36%) at an **11.3%** margin; EPS **5.74 SEK**. **[Fact]** Operating cash flow was **184.6 MSEK** (+92%); the board proposes **no dividend** for FY2025. Cash returns are modest; the model is reinvestment plus EPS growth.
why_market_wrong: Reported **organic net sales -5%** may anchor sentiment while margin and cash-flow inflection are under-discounted. **[Inference]** FY2025 EBITA margin rose from **9.6%** to **11.3%** and Q1 2026 EBITA margin hit a record **14.3%** (prior interim, inventory tier), suggesting pruning is working. There is no dated HK payoff; mispricing, if any, is franchise quality at a full multiple (~**15.7×** FY2025 FCF/share at **~158 SEK**).
